statute: Virginia Code § 54.1-1100 et seq. (Board for Contractors); § 54.1-1115 (penalties)
license_notes: Virginia requires ALL GC and specialty contractors to hold a state license. Three classes: Class A (up to $10,000 per project/$150,000/yr), Class B (up to $120,000/$750,000/yr), Class C (unlimited). License number AND expiration date must appear on mechanics liens.

mandatory_clauses.label: Virginia DPOR license disclosure
Right to cancel (home solicitation)
mandatory_clauses.text:
RIGHT TO CANCEL: You have the right to cancel this contract within three business days of signing. To cancel, send written notice to:
{{contractor_name}}, {{contractor_address}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Fine not to exceed $500 per day of violation. Class 1 misdemeanor — up to 1 year jail. No lien rights without license (AND must include license number + expiration on lien).
notes: Virginia requires state license for all GC and specialty work. Three classes based on annual revenue and project value. Electrical, plumbing, HVAC, gas fitting require separate trade endorsements. Mechanics lien must include license number AND expiration date — missing these invalidates the lien. Reciprocity with AL, DC, KY, MD, WV, and NC. Written contracts required for projects over $1,000.
